entry.title: Washington Post: 23 Jun, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • The E.U. has backed candidate status for Ukraine. Here’s what that means.
  • Russia-Ukraine war live updates: Ukraine granted E.U. membership candidacy
  • Traces of highly contagious poliovirus found in British sewage
  • E.U. leaders make Ukraine a candidate for membership
  • U.S. monkeypox response mirrors early coronavirus missteps, experts say
  • Thousands of ‘witches’ could be posthumously pardoned in Scotland
  • U.S. woman denied abortion in Malta to be airlifted out amid fears for her life
  • Afghans mourn the dead, search for shelter after devastating earthquake
  • Amid criticism, Western tourists returning to a battered Syria
  • After restaurant attack, authorities continue to gaslight China’s women
  • Sri Lanka’s economy has ‘completely collapsed,’ prime minister says
  • How a Russian investigative reporter found out he was a Kremlin target
  • Former Miss Brazil dead at 27 after having tonsils removed
  • A corruption inquiry ends in South Africa. Will justice follow?
  • Letters written, tanks in position as battle for Lysychansk looms
  • Maps of Russia’s invasion of Ukraine
  • Russia intensifies push to seize Luhansk
  • Pew poll: Global views on NATO and U.S. rise; for Russia, a decline
  • The scandal over Boris Johnson job-hunting for his wife won’t go away
  • Va. couple sailing to Portugal go missing after boat damaged in storm
  • Afghans bury dead, dig for survivors of devastating quake
  • Aid groups warn of grave consequences if Syria mandate stops
  • Spirit of Virgil Abloh lives on at Louis Vuitton in Paris
  • Commonwealth heads call for climate action at Rwanda meeting
  • Tanzania's Masaai demand Indigenous rights in UN framework
  • Lawmakers and mayors decry ‘reckless’ Supreme Court ruling on guns
  • Trump called Jan. 6 participants ‘smart,’ filmmaker says
  • Publix won’t offer coronavirus vaccines to children under 5 ‘at this time’
  • They protested with Pride flags at graduation. Their fight isn’t over.
  • 6 killed after ‘Huey’ helicopter crashes in West Virginia
  • Alexa has a new voice — your dead relative’s
  • A half-century after one movement, ‘Fierce Madres’ in Uvalde call for another
  • She had ‘soulmate’ kill her husband. A judge gave her life in prison.
  • He stole a patrol vehicle, officials say — then responded to an emergency
  • Jan. 6 hearing live updates: Panel to focus on Trump’s pressure on Justice Department to overturn election
  • Federal agents searched home of Trump Justice Dept. official Jeffrey Clark
  • As Biden touts gas tax pause, even some of his own officials balk
  • What to expect at Thursday’s Jan. 6 committee hearing
  • Post Politics Now: With new evidence coming in, Jan. 6 panel to continue hearings in July
  • W.Va. politician, abortion protester sentenced to 3 months for Jan. 6 role
  • Judge delays Proud Boys trial in fallout from House Jan. 6 hearings
  • Stocks vacillate as investors try to shake off recession fears
  • How a teen lifeguard took on the gender pay gap — and what she gained
  • Yelp shuts some offices doubling down on remote; CEO calls hybrid “hell”
  • Kyle Rittenhouse announces video game to fund media defamation suits
  • 2 huge chip makers warn of expansion delays as subsidies bill languishes
  • ‘F1 22’ roars into a new era with revamped cars and overhauled physics
  • Mortgage rates continue to escalate, move closer to 6 percent
  • Distinguished homes for sale in the D.C. region
  • How a ‘move manager’ can help you trim down on your belongings
  • What I love about my home in Portland, Ore.
  • The Rich-Poor Divide on Clean Power Is Getting Wider
  • The Latest Currency War May Just Be a Skirmish
  • Faster Progress Is Needed on Treatments for Long Covid
  • Five Notes on the Republican Party’s Future
  • What You Need to Know About Recessions — Including Whether We’re in One
  • Here’s How US States Differ on Carrying Guns in Public
  • What’s a Reverse Currency War and Who’s Fighting One?
  • These Are the Gun Measures Advancing in US Congress
  • Biden is under pressure to go soft on China’s genocide
  • Democrats are having a mini-2024 primary right now
  • Your questions about covid-19, answered by Dr. Leana Wen
  • The Jan. 6 hearings’ slippery slope
  • There is a better option to keep Trump out of office than prosecution
  • The Supreme Court supercharges the Second Amendment
  • D.C.'s promising approach to violence prevention has room for improvement
  • The Supreme Court’s gun ruling is bad, but not for the reasons you might think
  • Judge: Fox Corp. can’t wiggle out of Dominion’s ‘big lie’ lawsuit
  • The Supreme Court is ready to gut every blue state’s gun laws
  • The most dangerous gun ruling in history, at the worst possible time
  • Happy the elephant had her day in court. We humans are better for it.
  • Lithuanian President Gitanas Nauseda: Now is the time to make NATO even stronger
  • No separation of Christianity and state for this court
  • Why France’s overseas territories are voting for anyone but Macron
  • Biden must not betray defenders of democracy in Saudi Arabia
  • Ask Eugene Robinson about the latest news around the U.S. and world
  • Chat with Perry Bacon Jr. about politics and beyond
  • Chat with Jennifer Rubin about her columns, politics, policy and more
  • Send a letter to the editor
  • Contact the reader representative
  • Submit an op-ed
Breaking news and analysis on politics, business, world national news, entertainment more. In-depth DC, Virginia, Maryland news coverage including traffic, weather, crime, education, restaurant reviews and more.

entry.title: NY Times: 23 Jun, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Federal Authorities Search Home of Jeffrey Clark, Trump Justice Dept. Official
  • Alex Holder, Documentary Filmmaker, Emerges as Jan. 6 Witness
  • Here’s a timeline of Trump’s efforts to bend the Justice Department to his will.

  • FDA Orders Juul to Remove E-Cigarette Products from U.S. Market
  • One Button Could Have ‘Saved More Lives’ in Surfside Condo Collapse

  • Erriyon Knighton May Soon Be Faster Than Usain Bolt Ever Was
  • The Supreme Court Should Uphold New York’s Concealed Carry Law
  • Why One Progressive Public Defender Hoped for an N.R.A. Victory

  • Eric Greitens’s Plug for Political Violence Is No Joke
  • Patsy Mink’s Fight for Title IX
  • Conservatives Are Attempting a Broad Attack on Gay Rights and Culture
  • Title IX 50th Anniversary: We Need a New Deal for Women’s Sports

  • Pope Francis Will Visit Canada for Apology Despite Health Issues
  • Iran Dismisses Revolutionary Guards’ Powerful Intelligence Chief
  • Analysis: What Turkey Wants to Let Finland and Sweden Into NATO
  • ‘Not in This for the Money’: Why Some Families Sue North Korea
  • When, Exactly, Did Mount Vesuvius Erupt?
  • Austria quietly discards a vaccine mandate that it never enforced.
  • Biden Administration Tosses Trump Definition of ‘Habitat’ for Endangered Species
  • Deborah Birx Said Trump White House Asked Her to Water Down Covid Guidance to States
  • In a stricken village in Paktika, ‘Everyone here cried from night to morning.’
  • Russia is crimping gas flows just as Europe is racing to stock up for winter.
  • Biden’s Inner Circle Debates Future of Offshore Drilling
  • A Gay Pilot Reflects on What Travel Means to Queer Folks
  • European Leaders Meet to Decide on Granting Ukraine E.U. Candidate Status
  • As Russia Fortifies Ukrainian Nuclear Plant, Workers Flee Repression, Officials Say
  • Your Thursday Briefing
  • Russia Gains in Eastern Ukraine, Threatening to Overrun Luhansk
  • Devastating Afghanistan Earthquake Leaves More Than 1,000 Dead
  • Your Thursday Briefing: A Deadly Earthquake in Afghanistan

  • Liz Cheney Encourages Wyoming Democrats to Change Parties to Vote for Her
  • Their Baby Died. Then a Boston Hospital Lost the Body.
  • What Does the Supreme Court Ruling Mean for the New York Gun Law?
  • New Hampshire Poll Points to Trouble for Trump; Growing Support for DeSantis
  • Ohio State University Trademarks ‘The’
  • Extreme Heat Blankets U.S. from Minnesota to Deep South
  • What Are the Dirtiest Beaches in California?
  • In Photos: The Spectacle of the Jan. 6 Hearings Looms Over Washington
  • Jan. 6 Panel Says It Has New Evidence of Trump’s Pressure on Justice Dept.
  • G.O.P. Testimony at Jan. 6 Panel Exposes a Party Torn Between Truth and Trump
  • Louisiana Nursing Home Owner Faces Cruelty Charges in Deadly Hurricane Evacuation
  • Why Some Republicans Are Second-Guessing Boycotting the Jan. 6 Panel
  • G.O.P. Support Is Fragile on Bipartisan Gun Safety Bill
  • Many Russian Cyberattacks Failed in First Months of Ukraine War, Study Says
  • Inflation Complicates Biden’s Deliberations on Student Loan Forgiveness
  • Biden Just Proposed a Gas Tax Holiday. Will It Work?
  • As Midterms Loom, Mark Zuckerberg Shifts Focus Away From Elections
  • The Rise of the 0.5 Selfie
  • Fed officials pledge ‘unconditional’ dedication to lowering inflation.
  • Netflix Lays Off Another 300 Employees
  • Fed Officials Pledge ‘Unconditional’ Dedication to Lowering Inflation
  • Germany Warns of Gas Crisis After Russia Reduces Supply
  • Netflix Needs Some Help With Ads
  • 7-Eleven Franchisee Who Rebelled Against Company Loses in Court
  • What to See, Eat and Do in New Orleans
  • Art World Confronts Its Challenges
  • Cost of Owning a Home Surges Above the Cost of Renting One
  • What Fed Chair Powell has said about inflation, interest rates and the risk of a recession.

  • Russia Cuts Gas Flows as Europe Races to Stock Up for Winter

entry.title: The Sydney Morning Herald: 23 Jun, 2022
entry.summary: The Sydney Morning Herald: 23 Jun, 2022 published at 2022-06-23 19:03PM.
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Juul e-cigarettes banned in the US after FDA ruling
  • Lockdowns spark signs of defiance among China’s restless youth
  • I’m rejecting the style conventions that new mothers normally follow
  • Get planting: 10 of the most aromatic plants for winter
  • Who gets fired over social media posts? We studied hundreds of cases to find out
  • New data is ‘bad, bad’ diet news. Here are the changes to make now
  • Make or brake: The critical test for Albanese’s manufacturing vision
  • Australia has a lot to learn about renewable energy but maybe not from Germany
  • Steaks raised in top restaurants as main meals break $50 mark

  • Calls to expand access to oral COVID-19 drugs as expiry dates loom
  • Free shots fail to boost flu vaccination rates, as COVID hospitalisations rise

  • Regulators home in on banks’ skinny deposit rate rises
  • As a kid I was homeless, and that’s why I joined the Vinnies CEO Sleepout last night

entry.content:

entry.title: Guardian: 23 Jun, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • ‘Ukraine’s future is in the EU’: Zelenskiy welcomes granting of candidate status
  • Pope Francis orders online release of second world war-era ‘Jewish’ files
  • Russia-Ukraine war: EU leaders to decide on Kyiv bid; Russia aims to turn Donbas cities into a Mariupol, Zelenskiy says – live news
  • Serena Williams’ comeback cut short by Ons Jabeur injury at Eastbourne
  • Scottish islanders save US couple’s wedding after their luggage gets lost
  • New Zealand’s Mitchell and Blundell hold up England’s charge in Third Test
  • Scientists discover world’s largest bacterium, the size of an eyelash
  • Past and present collide as England face Netherlands in ‘special’ friendly
  • Appetite for frogs’ legs in France and Belgium ‘driving species to extinction’
  • Barcelona offer Bayern Munich initial €35m for Robert Lewandowski

  • Spread of ‘free-range’ farming may raise risk of animal-borne pandemics – study
  • ‘My film would not exist if the patriarchy did not exist’: the director taking on the Greek establishment

  • Miami condo collapse: victims and families to receive $1.02bn settlement
  • Germany moves closer to gas rationing as Russia chokes supplies
  • New York magazine’s ‘Canceled at 17’ isn’t just an article – it’s ammunition | Arwa Mahdawi
  • The dinner party from hell: Boris, Theresa and Dave | John Crace
  • Welcome to the fun house! Sharks, skaters and smelters liven up Documenta 15
  • Fighting entering ‘fearsome climax’ in key regions, says Ukraine
  • Does David Frost’s claim that Brexit is working stack up?
  • Travis Jay review – a loose, likable set about fathers and sons
  • Look around, the Great Conservative Experiment has failed in the UK | Andy Beckett
  • Japanese rockers Kikagaku Moyo: ‘Watching people board a train – that’s psychedelic!’
  • Messiaen: Vingt Regards sur l’Enfant-Jésus review | Andrew Clements's classical album of the week
  • Republican who livestreamed Capitol attack given three months in prison
  • ‘I stumbled across it’: the push to help Tanzania’s untapped football talent
  • The 20 best films set on trains – ranked!
  • US woman denied abortion in Malta flies to Spain to terminate pregnancy
  • Fright club: Brad Pitt’s GQ photoshoot is an embarrassment of pictures
  • ‘Everyone wants them’: the trainers that sold for $150,000
  • Why Gary Lineker was right to speak out about receiving racist abuse | Jason Okundaye
  • Haaland and Núñez deals will leave top two’s rivals wondering how to catch up | Karen Carney
  • ‘It stays mind-blowing’: Billie Eilish’s mum on her daughter making Glastonbury history
  • Fears for wolf population after ‘catastrophic’ wildfire in Spain
  • Dining across the divide: ‘There were moments when I wanted to press eject’
  • The Fisherman’s Hut: inside the luxury Russian dacha linked to Vladimir Putin
  • ‘Pretty damn cool’: Ellie Goulding on rewilding as a cure for our planet – and our mental health
  • ‘I chatted about the weather with Janet Jackson’: the Glastonbury festival I’ll never forget
  • Soccer Mommy: Sometimes, Forever review | Alexis Petridis's album of the week
  • Inca-era tomb unearthed beneath home in Peru’s capital
  • ‘Nobody called 911’: what can be done to change the culture of hazing at US colleges?
  • Britain’s vanishing rainforests must be protected, say campaigners
  • Growing pains?: How to stop yourself getting injured while gardening – from essential exercises to the perfect posture
  • Grounded! What to do in an airport if your flight is delayed by one, three, six or 12 hours
  • Putin is not yet ready to end the Ukraine war. When he is, we must be prepared | Jonathan Powell

entry.title: South China Morning Post: 23 Jun, 2022
entry.summary: South China Morning Post: 23 Jun, 2022 published at 2022-06-23 18:30PM.
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Hong Kong lawmakers, business leaders call for cutting hotel quarantine to 3 days after incoming leader John Lee reveals reopening options
  • Government efforts to spur acceptance of ethnic minority groups in Hong Kong kindergartens prove effective, as intake in preschools grow
  • Hong Kong’s public hospitals unveil scheme to hire doctors from Greater Bay Area in bid to ease manpower crunch
  • Lawmakers and engineers call for safety review of power cable bridges after fire led to Hong Kong’s worst outage in decades
  • Opening of Hong Kong Palace Museum a milestone in the Jockey Club’s support for the HKSAR in the past 25 years
  • Hong Kong police officer coerced teen into taking blame for school fire two years ago, judge rules
  • Hong Kong police arrest unemployed man over indecent assault of 12-year-old girl at park toilet
  • Coronavirus: infection of 2 top Hong Kong officials raises concerns over state leader visit for 25th anniversary of handover
  • Obesity among Hong Kong children increased during coronavirus pandemic due to unhealthy lifestyle, survey shows
  • Exclusive interview: Hong Kong’s leader-designate John Lee says ‘I am a pragmatic person’ and ‘I will be open to everybody’
  • HKMA intervenes to defend Hong Kong dollar peg, taking currency purchases beyond HK$100 billion amid capital flight
  • BN(O) scheme: more than 8,500 Hong Kong students estimated to have applied for school places in Britain since September, according to UK report
  • Exchange Fund: how US$574 billion war chest defeated Soros, defended Hong Kong dollar peg in post-handover financial crises
  • Why China should brace itself for a Pacific rebuff
  • Chinese radar breakthrough will see commercial drones soar in power and applications, says Shenzhen team
  • China’s aircraft carrier No 4 will not catch up with US Navy’s nuclear-powered giants, analysts say
  • China ‘commends’ Nepal’s decision to reject US partnership programme
  • US-China tech war: top Chinese scientist envisions forked RISC-V chip design standard to cushion decoupling impact
  • China floats BRICS free-trade deal, while Russia’s Vladimir Putin calls for an alternative to US dollar
  • China’s strict Covid-19 border controls show more signs of easing, as Zhejiang province cuts entry quarantine
  • China’s zero-Covid policy hammered Beijing’s economy in May. But how bad was it compared to Shanghai?
  • Chinese video gaming revenue falls in May, a third month of decline amid slowing economy and less player spending
  • ‘Mini hotels’ for sea creatures reveal Hong Kong’s rich marine life
  • China’s payments, fintech sectors to ‘play bigger role’ in boosting economy, President Xi says, in positive signal for Big Tech
  • Amid record-breaking floods in China, premier calls for stepped up disaster control and relief
  • Shenzhen tightens Covid-19 controls with 48-hour negative test result required to use public transport after three new cases found
  • China officials who abused health codes to stop bank protests punished
  • China can expect a ‘harder line’ from G7, Nato countries at summits
  • G7 pledges to counter ‘state-driven censorship’ and help protect journalists
  • Long Covid: children can have lingering symptoms but the chances are low, study finds
  • To keep up with China, Pentagon must cut red tape and embrace latest technology, top US official says
  • 6 ways China thinks it can offset zero-Covid impact to get its economy back on track
  • ‘It can create life’: Chinese team claim stem cell breakthrough in mice study
  • South China Sea: Philippines, China abandon joint energy exploration talks
  • Malaysia: Were Mahathir’s remarks about ‘reclaiming’ Singapore and Indonesia’s Riau Islands aimed at Johor’s sultan?
  • Malaysia’s role pressuring Myanmar junta praised by UN human rights expert
  • Okinawa wants US troops out – 77 years after bloody battle
  • Philippines: Making a splash in the ‘mermaiding’ subculture that’s got fans hooked worldwide
  • Bring cannabis to Indonesia and you could face death penalty, Thai embassy tells tourists
  • Inquiry finds Australian mining firms ignored ‘horrific’ sexual assaults against women
  • Malaysia’s palm oil output recovery hopes dashed as fertiliser, worker shortages persist
  • Are Singapore, Australia, Hong Kong expats still living the dream? Burnout, stress and loneliness rife since Covid-19, survey finds
  • How the US can help end Afghanistan’s humanitarian crisis
  • European Union agrees to make Ukraine a candidate for EU membership
  • Americans have right to carry guns in public, says US Supreme Court in landmark decision
  • British Airways set for ‘summer of strikes’ at London’s Heathrow
  • US$156 million Russian superyacht in Dubai tests US-UAE ties amid sanctions
  • Instagram to protect young users with new AI tool to verify age
  • Ukraine: Russian soldier rape trial begins, first of many cases
  • EU leaders to set war-torn Ukraine on road to membership
  • Russian TV protester Marina Ovsyannikova in exile says caught in ‘information war’
  • Afghanistan earthquake: Rescuers dig by hand as UN, US and EU offer help
  • Ghislaine Maxwell deserves 30-55 years in prison: US prosecutors
  • Polio back in UK after virus detected in London sewage samples
  • Vienna returns as world’s ‘most liveable city’; Ukraine’s Kyiv not on list amid war
  • US Capitol riot hearings to take break as new evidence floods in
  • Russian troops ‘executed’ photographer in Ukraine, press group says
  • Russia’s Sergey Lavrov in Iran to discuss nuclear deal, cooperation
  • Rupert Murdoch and Jerry Hall are getting a divorce, report says
  • Look to portfolio resilience in times of volatility
  • What the bitcoin bloodbath reveals about the cryptocurrency market
  • Hong Kong audit regulator in talks with Beijing’s Ministry of Finance about granting it access to working papers of mainland Chinese companies, chairman says
  • In boost for Hong Kong buyers, UK home prices have trailed those in EU states in six years since Brexit vote
  • China considers extending EV tax exemption to put industry back on track after Covid-19 lockdown
  • China bans over 30 live-streaming behaviours, demands qualifications to discuss law, finance, medicine
  • Hong Kong, 25 years after its return to China, needs a new narrative, not nostalgia
  • Tesla battery supplier CATL raises US$6.7 billion in Shenzhen stock placement as mainland China’s capital market thrives
  • Alibaba, Geely lift Hong Kong stocks on China stimulus bets as Xi pledges to achieve 5.5 per cent growth target
  • China’s appetite for protein developed through fermentation grows, amid drive to boost demand for meat alternatives, net-zero goal
  • Hongkongers spoilt for choice in Lohas Park, as developers offer lower prices for new projects to counter interest rate rises
  • Citigroup predicts 16 per cent upside in Hang Seng Index as corporate earnings, China vaccine progress revive market confidence
  • Amazon has a plan to make Alexa mimic anyone’s voice
  • Google faces second turnover fine in Russia over failure to delete banned content about Ukraine
  • VELA CASA showcases charismatic, art-level European designer brand furniture
  • Netflix K-drama review: Money Heist: Korea – Joint Economic Area Part 1 gives a slick geopolitical makeover to hit Spanish thriller series
  • Japan embraces CBD sold in drinks, candies and vapes at cafes and health stores, but keeps strict laws on marijuana’s recreational use
  • Hong Kong Fringe Club mounts what could be its farewell show, a retrospective of art exhibitions from the past four decades
  • K-drama review: Woori the Virgin – bubbly romcom starring Lim Soo-hyang recovers for a romantic photo finish
  • Hong Kong-style milk tea brand Hoko in London is giving emigrants a welcome taste of home
  • How Hong Kong’s arts scene has changed in 25 years and the challenges to come as its cultural planning becomes more aligned with mainland China
  • The sinking of Hong Kong’s Jumbo Floating Restaurant – bane or blessing?
  • Quarantine spawned a cookbook, washouts bred dining pop-ups, and she launched a handmade sauce business in Hong Kong mid-pandemic – Simran Savlani has been busy
  • For Singapore’s fashion designers, post-Covid recovery rests on drawing customers from overseas
  • Meet Jude Law’s mirror-image son Rafferty – from modelling for Dolce & Gabbana and campaigning for PETA, to acting in Masters of the Air with Austin Butler, he could become just as famous as his dad
  • Are Austin Butler and Kaia Gerber showbiz’s hottest couple? The Elvis star and supermodel have flaunted their relationship on red carpets from the Met Gala to Cannes, and mum Cindy Crawford approves
  • Meet Elon Musk’s first wife Justine Wilson – the fantasy author was married to the billionaire Tesla CEO for 8 years, and is supporting their twin child Vivian, who just came out as a trans woman
  • Milan Men’s Fashion Week: Zegna flaunted light, minimalist pieces connected to the earth – tying in with the luxury brand’s sustainable materials and practices
  • Inside Asia’s obsession with Boys’ Love TV shows: queer series like Thailand’s 2Gether and South Korea’s Semantic Error have millions of fans, social media groups, YouTube channels and LGBT podcasts
  • Milan Men’s Fashion Week: Giorgio Armani mixed elegance with a touch of whimsy through colour blocking and surprising prints for spring/summer 2023 in shades of blue
  • Meet Jeon Jong-seo, Money Heist Korea’s rising star: she acted with Zac Efron and Kate Hudson, her first film was shortlisted for an Oscar before Parasite – and she’s dating her former director
  • STYLE Edit: Gucci’s Landmark Hong Kong flagship store unveils its swanky makeover to offer the Gucci DIY service, and an exclusive VIP lounge and bar for clients
  • British royals, undercover: Queen Elizabeth, Prince Harry, Meghan Markle, Prince William and Kate Middleton have all gone incognito – and Princess Diana even crashed a gay bar with Freddie Mercury
SCMP.com, Hong Kong's premier online English daily provides exclusive up-to-date news, audio video news, podcasts, RSS Feeds, Blogs, breaking news, top stories, award winning news and analysis on Hong Kong and China.

entry.title: Financial Times: 23 Jun, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Germany moves closer to gas rationing over Russian disruption
  • Russia’s gas squeeze: a moment of truth for Europe
  • G7 leaders to debate ways to stabilise global energy markets
  • Biden’s blues: is the US president out of political capital?
  • Crypto enthusiasts are betting the house on creative destruction
  • Index rejigs: demotion would teach Meta the value of humility
  • Live news updates: Zelenskyy criticises Israel for not sanctioning Russia nor providing weaponry
  • Government bonds rally on fears of global economic slowdown
  • Supreme Court recognises right to carry firearms in public and strikes down NY law
  • Why New York gun violence victims may have their day in court
  • FDA bans Juul products from US market in blow to e-cigarette maker
  • Altria: teen-appeal of menthol vapes means Juul is no longer minted
  • Ken Griffin’s Citadel ditches Chicago for Miami following crime complaints
  • She was loved for standing up to China. She may die in jail
  • How ‘vice-signalling’ swallowed electoral politics
  • Christian Sewing waives 1.4% of his bonus over Deutsche’s use of unapproved messaging apps
  • Support grows among Fed officials for further 0.75 percentage point rate rise
  • Norway makes surprise 50 basis point rise
  • Ronaldo signs NFT deal with crypto exchange Binance
  • Quant hedge funds profit from cryptocurrency turmoil
  • Toyota recalls EV fleet as challenge to Tesla dominance suffers setback
  • US banks take a hit from discounted corporate bond sales
  • Copper price at 16-month low as slowdown fears hit industrial metals
  • Broadcom’s $69bn VMware deal set for lengthy EU antitrust investigation
  • ‘People are hungry’: food crisis starts to bite across Africa
  • World’s biggest bacteria discovered in Caribbean mangrove swamp
  • Inflation eats into eurozone business activity
  • Top EU lawmaker calls for ‘hard look’ at budgeting cycle as crises test bloc
  • UK prepares to include over-50s in autumn Covid booster campaign
  • UK faces summer of travel chaos as strike threats spread
  • Eurozone central bank chiefs warn on soaring inflation expectations
  • UK explores 5% pay rises for public sector workers
  • EU aims to de-escalate tensions over Russian trade to Kaliningrad
  • If a nimble UK is to be better regulated than the EU it has to be just that — nimble
  • What Russia’s fortress balance sheet can tell us about the dollar’s future
  • UK auditors told to be more sceptical when assessing company accounts
  • Toshiba: weak yen strengthens hand of foreign bidders
  • Why it’s getting harder to kick our fossil fuel addiction
  • FirstFT: Powell warns recession ‘a possibility’
  • Inflation drives surge in interest payments on UK public debt
  • Polymetal appoints new auditor to replace Deloitte
  • Australia mines inquiry finds ‘prevalent’ sexual abuse of women
  • Ireland’s AIB fined record €96.7mn over tracker mortgage scandal
  • Nato must show Putin it is serious about defence
  • Supply won’t save us
  • Complacency led policymakers to misdiagnose inflation
  • Can all of Africa get access to electricity?
  • Biden’s proposed ‘gas tax holiday’ runs into opposition
  • SumUp/FalconX: escalating uprounds will feel the downdraft of recession
  • Britain’s tech sector still believes in opportunity in adversity
  • TV writer Peter Kosminsky: ‘You see the effects of cyber warfare everywhere’
  • Lessons for Elon Musk from Meta’s content moderation
  • Chinese memory chip king’s rapid growth, Musk’s China challenges
  • SumUp struggles to €8bn valuation as tech sell-off hits UK fintech
  • ‘Celsians’ offer an interesting new way to lose money
  • Ecommerce’s low stocks, high ambitions
  • South Korea metaverse ETF performance slumps
  • How the revolution in electric vehicles is changing mining
  • Singapore regulator vows to be ‘unrelentingly hard’ on crypto
  • Investors crank up bets on BoJ surrendering yield curve controls
  • US stock rally fades as oil price drop drags down energy companies
  • UK Infrastructure Bank pledges to make direct equity investments
  • My hot take on the future of heatwaves
  • Don’t blame market forces for female tennis stars being short-changed
  • Monte dei Paschi: spot the snag with a turnround reliant on low bad loans
  • Reflections on France after the elections
  • Gong-worthy dining at a modernist masterpiece
  • Taiwanese compulsory military service should be improved, not extended
  • Collective pensions: there’s little protection in the crowd
  • What business can learn from the UK’s public sector strikes
Financial Times

entry.title: Asahi Shimbun: 23 Jun, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • USB devices with personal data of all Amagasaki residents lost
  • Tokyo confirms 1st week-on-week rise in COVID-19 cases in 5 weeks
  • First tour group from Hong Kong in 2 years lands at Narita Airport

  • Women account for record 33% of candidates in Diet election

  • Myanmar’s Suu Kyi moved from secret location to prison

  • EDITORIAL: China’s reckless military buildup is needlessly stoking tensions
  • VOX POPULI: Recalling Battle of Okinawa via the stark account of a U.S. Marine
The Asahi Shimbun is widely regarded for its journalism as the most respected daily newspaper in Japan. The English version offers selected articles from the vernacular Asahi Shimbun, as well as extensive coverage of cool Japan,focusing on manga, travel and other timely news.

entry.title: NY Times (Print): 23 Jun, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Biden Pushes Congress for Three-Month Gas Tax Holiday
  • Gun Bill’s Progress Reflects Political Shift, but G.O.P. Support Is Fragile
  • A Year Later, Some Republicans Second-Guess Boycotting the Jan. 6 Panel
  • Linchpin of Ukrainian Defiance, a Southern City Endures Russian Barrage
  • Devastating Afghanistan Earthquake Leaves More Than 1,000 Dead
  • Could New York City Lose Its Last Remaining Jewish Congressman?
  • Penélope Cruz, Antonio Banderas and Their ‘Official Competition’
  • ‘Corsicana’ Review: Four Lost Hearts in the Heart of Texas

  • The Government Set a Colossal Wildfire. What Are Victims Owed?
  • M.T.A. Vows to Make Subways 95% Accessible. It Will Take 33 Years.
  • Powell says the Fed is ‘not trying to provoke’ a recession, but it is ‘certainly a possibility.’
  • Russia Crimps Gas Flows Just as Europe Races to Stock Up for Winter

  • A Big Step Toward Fusion Energy Is Hailed by a Seattle Start-Up
  • Chinese Businessman Tied to Alibaba Rape Case Is Sentenced to Prison

  • Labor Agency Seeks Broad Order Against Starbucks in Federal Court

  • Andrew Gillum, DeSantis’s 2018 Rival, Is Charged With Conspiracy and Fraud
  • Ocasio-Cortez Endorses Insurgent in New York Lt. Governor’s Race
  • Vaccinations for children under 5 start in New York City, including in Times Square.
